body:
### User Story
As a <user role>, I want to modify the boot sequence messages in a simple markdown file and support dynamic variables (browser, viewport, IP) so that I can easily customize the first impression of the website and make it feel more interactive.

### Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Boot messages are read from `content/boot.md`.
- [ ] The `BootSequence` component supports placeholders for `{{browser}}`, `{{viewport}}`, `{{ip}}`, `{{os}}`, and `{{date}}`.
- [ ] Placeholders are replaced with real user data at runtime.
- [ ] The existing "click to skip" functionality is preserved.
- [ ] The boot sequence timing remains consistent.

### Technical Notes
- Pass the boot markdown content from server to client via props.
- Implement a regex-based replacement utility for the variables.
- Detect user agent, screen size, and system info on the client.
- IP detection might require a client-side API call or server-side header injection if available.
title: [Feature]: Dynamic Boot Sequence from Markdown with Variable Support
